verses.text: 'Knowledge puffeth up, but charity edifieth'; he that loves God is known of Him. Love over knowledge. Mere knowledge inflates; love builds up.
'Knowledge puffeth up, but charity edifieth'; he that loves God is known of Him. Love over knowledge. Mere knowledge inflates; love builds up.
'Knowledge puffeth up, but charity edifieth'; he that loves God is known of Him. Love over knowledge. Mere knowledge inflates; love builds up.
An idol is nothing; 'to us there is but one God, the Father... and one Lord Jesus Christ.' One God. The believer knows idols are empty.
An idol is nothing; 'to us there is but one God, the Father... and one Lord Jesus Christ.' One God. The believer knows idols are empty.
An idol is nothing; 'to us there is but one God, the Father... and one Lord Jesus Christ.' One God. The believer knows idols are empty.
Not all have this knowledge; some, eating, are defiled in conscience; 'meat commendeth us not to God.' Tender conscience. Food itself neither helps nor harms the soul.
Not all have this knowledge; some, eating, are defiled in conscience; 'meat commendeth us not to God.' Tender conscience. Food itself neither helps nor harms the soul.
Take heed lest your liberty 'become a stumblingblock to them that are weak'; shall the weak perish through thy knowledge? Care for the weak. Liberty must not wound a brother.
Take heed lest your liberty 'become a stumblingblock to them that are weak'; shall the weak perish through thy knowledge? Care for the weak. Liberty must not wound a brother.
Take heed lest your liberty 'become a stumblingblock to them that are weak'; shall the weak perish through thy knowledge? Care for the weak. Liberty must not wound a brother.
Sinning against the brethren, 'ye sin against Christ'; I will eat no flesh rather than offend. Self-denying love. He will surrender his right for a brother's sake.
